mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior month lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so a downward move in policy rates predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average after FEDFUNDS declines over the prior month, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and slower demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not negative at conventional significance.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while HOUST remains weak, retail demand is not translating into broad housing-led expansion, so equity gains should be limited and forward returns over the next 20 trading days turn negative.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and weak HOUST does not improve short-horizon downside prediction versus either series alone, or if SPX returns are not lower in the interaction state.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained pickup in INDPRO indicates stronger real activity and operating leverage for cyclicals, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive after two-month INDPRO rises, or if the signal loses predictive power in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDPC1 signals broader real demand growth that supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth fails to associate with higher 60-day forward SPX returns than negative or flat growth, or if the sign flips persistently across rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is still elevated and FEDFUNDS remains restrictive, real rates stay tight and equity valuations compress, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days should be negative.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL remains above its 6-month average and FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined high-inflation and high-policy-rate state does not produce below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not negative in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Stable PAYEMS with low UNRATE indicates resilient labor income and consumption, which supports S&P 500 performance over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises or stays flat while UNRATE does not rise
failure_condition: Reject if the stable-labor condition does not predict positive 20-day SPX returns better than unconditional averages, or if weak labor states perform equally well.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in HOUST after prior weakness signals a housing rebound that improves cyclical sentiment and should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises at least 5% from its prior 3-month low
failure_condition: Reject if housing rebound triggers are followed by non-positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional mean return is indistinguishable from zero.
